g.text: ! agent-infra investigation Survey existing CLI coding-agent harnesses and ide...
! cgl-publish systems Build cgl-publish primitive: one-shot publish of a...
**Recommendation: merge** — I agree with the claw's own assessment.
Objective: Build cgl-publish primitive: one-shot publish of a surface to the CDN with
- Two high-confidence claims cover the core contract: upload correctness (Claim 001) and
bundle traceability via CDN path schema (Claim 002).
- Both claims have concrete support: dry-run against `studio-cdn-staging` produced exit 0, an
live-URL → artifact-bundle invariant the orientation asked for.
- Shellcheck clean, 5 bats tests passing — meets the orientation's `require_provenance`
- Stop rule (`director_review_after_first_spec`) is satisfied: this is the first builder claw,
evidence is in, awaiting your call.
ID ROLE RECOMMENDATION CLAIMS EVENTS STATUS
- No live (non-dry-run) upload was exercised. Dry-run validates the manifest path but not
actual R2 auth + write. A follow-on smoke claw against staging would close that gap.
- Rollback is deferred to a follow-on (claw recommendation #3). That's reasonable for a
one-shot primitive but means a botched publish currently has no in-tool remediation.
- Wiring into `surface-snake` (recommendation #2) is gated on that lab's builder promotion —
Press 'c' to ask the chat about this lab • Esc or Enter to collapse
